data.title: Fusion Fuel to acquire 19 high-quality uranium & gas royalties in the Americas from Royal Uranium Inc. in share exchange deal.
data.description: On February 18, 2026, Dublin-based Fusion Fuel Green PLC revealed through a press release that they have finalized an agreement to acquire a majority stake in Royal Uranium Inc. This acquisition includes a diverse range of uranium royalties across exploration and development stages of the mining industry. Fusion Fuel, traded on NASDAQ under symbol HTOO, operates as a comprehensive green energy company, offering engineering, distribution, and sustainable solutions. This move signifies a strategic share exchange deal between the two entities, potentially leading to significant developments in the green energy and mining sectors.

data.title: NevGold discovers high-grade oxide Au-Ag at Bullet Zone, drilling 8.51 g/t Oxide AuEq over 10.6m, & 2.32 g/t Oxide AuEq over 86.8m.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on Feb. 18, 2026, NevGold Corp. ("NevGold" or the "Company") announced a significant discovery at its Limousine Butte Project in Nevada. The company identified high-grade oxide gold-antimony at the "Armory Fault" in the Bullet Zone. This discovery marks a crucial advancement in understanding the structural controls of mineralization in the area. During the 2H-2025 drill program, all drillholes at the Bullet Zone revealed substantial oxide gold-antimony mineralization, expanding the mineralization footprint. NevGold is pursuing a dual approach to project development, focusing on advancing near-term antimony production from historical gold leach pads while also conducting further drilling to establish an initial gold-antimony Mineral Resource Estimate ("MRE"). The company's stock is listed as TSXV: NAU, OTCQX: NAUFF, and Frankfurt: 5E50.

data.title: Clean Harbors (NYSE: CLH) Q4 earnings report: EPS of $1.62 beat estimates by 1.25%, revenue increased by $69.00 million.
data.description: Clean Harbors (NYSE: CLH) released its Q4 earnings report on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, at 07:30 AM. In the report, Clean Harbors exceeded estimated earnings by 1.25%, posting an EPS of $1.62 compared to the estimated $1.60. The company saw an increase in revenue by $69.00 million from the previous year. In the last quarter, Clean Harbors fell short on earnings. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Wingstop Inc. had a record 493 net new openings in 2025, 19.2% unit growth, and achieved 12.1% system-wide sales growth.
data.description: In 2025, Wingstop Inc. reported a record-breaking 493 net new openings, reflecting a remarkable 19.2% unit growth. This growth contributed to a 12.1% increase in system-wide sales for the fiscal year. The company introduced its 2026 guidance, showcasing promising prospects for the future. The announcement was made in Dallas on February 18, 2026.

data.title: Singular Genomics launches G4X™ in the U.S., offering high-throughput spatial technologies for precision medicine.
data.description: On February 18, 2026, Singular Genomics unveiled G4X, its latest high-throughput spatial technology aimed at enhancing precision medicine. The company, based in San Diego, announced the commercial release of G4X, making it available in the United States. This release signifies a significant advancement in enabling large-scale spatial analysis in the field of precision medicine.

data.title: Magaya Corporation promotes Matthew Fotouhi to Chief Innovation Officer (CInO) to drive enterprise-wide innovation.
data.description: On February 18, 2026, in Miami, Magaya Corporation, a top freight management platform for freight forwarders and customs brokers, revealed the appointment of Matthew Fotouhi as Chief Innovation Officer (CInO). This move extends Fotouhi's leadership within the organization, emphasizing his role in driving innovation.

data.title: Bitcoin holds steady at $67,000 amidst volatility; $188.97 million liquidations in 24hrs. ETF flows: BTC -$104.9M, ETH +$48.6M. Regulatory optimism on CLARITY Act. News source: Benzinga.com.
data.description: Bitcoin is hovering around $67,000 despite recent volatility and significant ETF withdrawals, with liquidations reaching $188.97 million in the last day. Investors are hopeful about potential advancements with the CLARITY Act for clearer crypto regulations in the U.S. Bitcoin ETFs experienced $104.9 million in outflows on Tuesday, while Ethereum ETFs saw $48.6 million in inflows. Prices for top cryptocurrencies are: Bitcoin at $67,332.41, Ethereum at $1,982.82, Solana at $83.54, XRP at $1.47, Dogecoin at $0.1002, and Shiba Inu at $0.56446. The meme coin sector is relatively stable, declining by 0.9% in the last 24 hours. More details can be found in the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: WLFI surges over 10% before Mar-a-Lago event. World Liberty Financial gains traction with $500M UAE deal controversy.
data.description: The DeFi project World Liberty Financial, supported by Trump, has experienced an increase in value due to significant purchases by large investors and a short squeeze. This comes as the project faces scrutiny over a deal worth $500 million with the UAE.

data.title: FDA grants Breakthrough Device status to NG Biotech and Hardy Diagnostics for 2 rapid tests targeting drug-resistant pathogens, aiding in the fight against Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR).
data.description: On February 18, 2026, NG Biotech and Hardy Diagnostics revealed a significant advancement in combating Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) by receiving FDA approval for their Breakthrough Device. This partnership, based in Santa Maria, Calif., and Guipry, France, marks a crucial development in the fight against AMR. The collaboration aims to address the growing concern of drug resistance.

The company surpassed estimated earnings by 64.1%, posting an EPS of $-0.14 compared to the expected $-0.39. Revenue saw a substantial increase of $139.14 million from the previous year. data.title: Sharon AI lists on Nasdaq with $125M IPO, offering 4,166,666 shares at $30 each.
data.description: SharonAI Holdings Inc. and its subsidiaries have revealed the pricing of their initial public offering in the United States. The offering consists of 4,166,666 shares of its Class A Ordinary Common Stock at a public price of $30.00 per share, resulting in total gross proceeds of around $125 million. This amount is calculated before accounting for underwriting discounts, commissions, and other offering expenses.

data.title: Starlab Space and Helogen collaborate to enhance life sciences research in microgravity, boosting scalability and regulatory-grade manufacturing, advancing discoveries.
data.description: Houston-based Starlab Space LLC has revealed a collaboration with Helogen Corporation to enhance low Earth orbit research. This partnership aims to revolutionize life sciences studies in microgravity by transitioning from individual experiments to larger, continuous discoveries. By teaming up with Helogen Corporation, a specialist in automated industrial processes for space, Starlab Space LLC looks to enable scalable manufacturing and autonomous biological operations in orbit. This strategic alliance signifies a significant step forward in advancing access to space research opportunities.

data.title: PROG Holdings, Inc. reports Q4 2025 results, showing strength & resilience. CEO Steve Michaels happy with disciplined execution.
data.description: In Salt Lake City, PROG Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:PRG) revealed financial results for the fourth quarter ending on December 31, 2025. The fintech holding company overseeing Progressive Leasing, Purchasing Power, Four Technologies, and MoneyApp reported a successful Q4 and full-year 2025. CEO Steve Michaels highlighted the company's disciplined execution and the robustness of PROG's multi-product platform amid a tough retail landscape and external factors. The quarter showcased the company's resilience, portraying a positive outlook for PROG Holdings.
